name: Milton Delugg
abstract: Milton Delugg (December 2, 1918 – April 6, 2015) was an American musician, composer and arranger.

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee does not mention Milton DeLugg; the name is only mentioned by the interviewer in the context of Tommy Newsom's employment.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: God I had seen Milton playing with Manny Klein and Matty Malneck... I think that was where Milton got his start
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Watrous
about_quotes.evidence: I was doing something for Milton DeLugg, who was the leader, and I did it and he fell down laughing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: Milton DeLugg was his conductor. Then I subbed over there when Jack Parr took over
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
